title: Rho HQ Office Redesign — 325 Pacific
role: On-site operations lead for multi-floor commercial office construction
description: Managing a full office build-out at 325 Pacific Ave, SF — coordinating across general contractors, electricians, flooring crews, design firms, and internal leadership. Own the critical path from demolition through move-in, resolving scheduling conflicts between concurrent workstreams across 3 floors.
key_highlights: Coordinating 15+ vendors simultaneously (electrical, flooring, HVAC, AV, lighting, millwork, cleaning)
Discovered structural blocker on Floor 3 that made planned electrical work infeasible — researched and proposed surface-mounted Connect Track alternative
Managed vendor dependencies: flooring can't start until electrical drilling is phased; lighting can't install after flooring is glued (no lift access)
Identified billing discrepancies on design firm invoices by cross-referencing billed meeting hours against calendar records
Built Notion-based project tracking system with Granola integration for real-time meeting capture

title: 325 Pacific Office Build-Out
role: On-site operations lead for multi-floor commercial office construction
description: Managing a full office build-out at 325 Pacific Ave, SF — coordinating across general contractors, electricians, flooring crews, design firms (OTS/Tangible Space), and internal leadership. Own the critical path from demolition through move-in, resolving scheduling conflicts between concurrent workstreams across 3 floors.
key_highlights: Coordinating 15+ vendors simultaneously (electrical, flooring, HVAC, AV, lighting, millwork, cleaning)
Discovered structural blocker on Floor 3 that made planned electrical work infeasible — researched and proposed surface-mounted Connect Track alternative
Managed vendor dependencies: flooring can't start until electrical drilling is phased; lighting can't install after flooring is glued (no lift access)
Identified billing discrepancies on design firm invoices by cross-referencing billed meeting hours against calendar records
Built Notion-based project tracking system with Granola integration for real-time meeting capture
milestones_completed: Basement fully reorganized into categorized sections
Phone booth chairs built and staged
Bathroom vanity sinks and towel dispensers installed (1st floor)
Floor 3 poke-through blocker resolved — holes drilled, cleanup done
Conference table poke-through resolved — alternate route through floor joist
Floor plugs demolished on Floor 1 (cleared for flooring)
First floor flooring demo completed (both areas)
Danny's flooring crew on-site and actively working
Basement circuit tracing complete — two dedicated circuits identified
Conference room power upgrade underway — two dedicated circuits from panel
New engineered lighting drawings received (improved circuit load distribution)
Cafe table spec changed from hardwired to plug-in undermount receptacles
GE Profile Opal 2.0 ice machine ordered (sub-$1K appliance spend)

title: Vendor & Budget Operations
description: Built and manage the full vendor lifecycle — from onboarding and insurance/COI compliance to invoice auditing and contract negotiation. Created accountability systems that caught billing discrepancies and saved budget.
key_highlights: Audited design firm invoice (#1581) by cross-referencing billed meeting hours against actual calendar events
Reviewed office lease to extract COI/insurance requirements and building rules
Negotiated vendor contracts: Bevy sparkling water (sub-$500/mo), Commonwealth Joe cold brew kegerator
Bevi Stand Up 2.0 sparkling water trial negotiated and contract sent via IntelAgree (7-day trial, 8 flavors)
Built vendor database in Notion for tracking contacts, contracts, and compliance status

title: Internal Comms & Employee Experience
role: Internal communications and workplace culture lead
description: Design and execute employee experience programming including events, internal newsletters, wellness initiatives, and surprise-and-delight activations across global offices.
key_highlights: Created 'Rhoice' internal Slack newsletter for AI tips initiative ($100 incentive per tip shared)
Organized SF team happy hour at Moongate Lounge, Chinatown
Planned themed office activations: World Health Day, Earth Day, Pretzel Day
Executed Global Health Day activation — pressed juice shots + self-care bags
Executed weekly treat drops (churro tots with signage/labels)
Coordinated Global Health Challenge / Strava step competition across offices (pushed to June)
Weekly surprise-and-delight treat program for office culture

title: Office Operations Playbook
role: Process builder for scalable office operations
description: Developing repeatable processes and documentation for standing up new office locations — covering shipping, building policies, vendor setup, and space planning. Applied across SF and NYC offices.
key_highlights: Created SF Building Policies guide with regulations and step-by-step instructions
Built shipping instructions documentation per office location
Established construction sync cadence with leadership (weekly calls + Slack updates)
Notion-based project tracking with multiple database views (tasks, projects, status board)

title: Aesthetic & Space Design
role: Space planning and design coordination lead
description: Coordinated the complete aesthetic transformation of Rho's 325 Pacific office across 3 floors — translating design firm concepts into actionable contractor specs and on-the-ground execution. Managed material selections, fixture specifications, lighting design, branding elements, and spatial layout decisions while balancing budget constraints and aggressive construction timelines.
key_highlights: Designed open office floor plan on Floor 3: sales floor, conference rooms, kitchen, phone booths
Transformed Floor 1 into communal co-working/client event space featuring exposed brick walls
Selected and coordinated 45 pendant light fixtures with Lutron Vive wireless 4-zone switch system
Chose perforated black metal panels (1/8" round hole pattern) for glass partitions — balancing privacy and transparency
Managed kitchen design: white modern cabinetry, dark countertops, blue accent lighting, stainless appliances
Coordinated building signage: illuminated blade sign (exterior), metal Rho logo with LED backlighting (interior), vinyl on partitions
Placed 7 phone booths around existing furniture layout
Selected vinyl flooring (rubber vinyl overlay) across all floors
Made on-site material and placement decisions in real-time when design firm wasn't present
